title: Presigned Downloads, CloudFront Signatures, Cookies, and Revocation · Runtime Contract: Signed Access Revocation
summary: A product places long-lived S3 presigned URLs in logs, uses one shared CloudFront key, signs with legacy assumptions, and promises immediate revocation after links escape. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the signed access revocation increment in time-bounded signed-delivery decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidenc…
objectives: Model expiry-at-request-start, range continuation, key-group rotation, authorization changes, logs and referrers, cache behavior, and emergency revocation limits.
Use CloudFront sign 1.11.7 with reviewed RSA or ECDSA keys and explicit HashSHA256 support while preserving compatibility evidence for zero-value SHA-1 behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Signed Access Revocation · Theory Signed Access Revocation
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 9794 during a cost-anomaly investigation. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound signed access by time, resource, key group, and explicit SHA-256 review; record cleanup, rollback, restoration, and residual-risk ownership; then cancel halfway through and prove every body, part, and goroutine reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Model expiry-at-request-start, range continuation, key-group rotation, authorization changes, logs and referrers, cache behavior, and emergency revocation limits.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Signed Access Revocation contract for the supplied data.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using model and expiry plus specific case evidence.
